title: Sign convention
description: Let's practice problems involving the Cartesian sign convention for mirrors and lenses.

assessment_items.question_content:
A ball, labelled $\text O$, is placed $9.0\ \text{cm}$ in front of a concave mirror, and $5.0\ \text{cm}$ below the principal axis. A real image, labelled $\text I$, is formed $4.5\ \text{cm}$ in front of the mirror and $2.5\ \text{cm}$ above the principal axis, as shown. The focus is $3.0\ \text{cm}$ away from the pole.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Choose the correct sign for each distance according to the sign convention for mirrors.**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
The ball is placed to the left of the mirror, so light is incident on the mirror towards the right.

##Horizontal distances

- The side of the mirror $\text{\greenD{in the direction}}$ of incident light is considered $\text{\greenD{positive}}$ (right of $\text P$ in this case).
- The side of the mirror $\text{\redD{in the opposite direction}}$ of incident light is considered $\text{\redD{negative}}$ (left of $\text P$ in this case).

##Vertical distances

- Distances measured $\text{\greenD{above}}$ the principal axis are taken to be $\text{\greenD{positive}}$ (image height in this case).
- Distances measured $\text{\redD{below}}$ the principal axis are taken to be $\text{\redD{negative}}$ (object height in this case).

While light from the ball is incident to the right, the ball $\text O$, the image $\text I$, and the focus $\text F$ are all to the left of the mirror. So their distances from the mirror get negative signs.

The image forms above the principal axis, so its height from the principal axis gets a positive sign.

The correct answer is:

||
- | :-:
$\text{Object distance}$ | $(-)$
$\text{Focal length}$ | $(-)$
$\text{Image height}$ | $(+)$

assessment_items.question_content:
Seema places a pencil on the principal axis of a concave mirror in such a way that the mirror forms a real, inverted image below the principal axis, as shown.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Choose the correct sign for each distance according to the sign convention for mirrors.**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
The pencil is placed to the left of the mirror, so light is incident on the mirror towards the right.

##Horizontal distances

- The side of the mirror $\text{\greenD{in the direction}}$ of incident light is considered $\text{\greenD{positive}}$ (right of $\text P$ in this case).
- The side of the mirror $\text{\redD{in the opposite direction}}$ of incident light is considered $\text{\redD{negative}}$ (left of $\text P$ in this case).

##Vertical distances

- Distances measured $\text{\greenD{above}}$ the principal axis are taken to be $\text{\greenD{positive}}$ (object height in this case).
- Distances measured $\text{\redD{below}}$ the principal axis are taken to be $\text{\redD{negative}}$ (image height in this case).

While light from the pencil (the object) is incident to the right, the object $\text O$, the image $\text I$, and the focus $\text F$ are all to the left of the mirror. So their distances from the mirror get negative signs.

The correct answer is:

||
- | :-:
$\text{Object distance}$ | $(-)$
$\text{Focal length}$ | $(-)$
$\text{Object height}$ | $(+)$

options.caption: The object is labelled $\text O$, and the image is labelled $\text I$.

assessment_items.question_content:
An upright object, $\text O$, is placed to the right of a concave mirror, above the principal axis. It forms a smaller, inverted image, $\text I$, below the principal axis, as shown.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Choose the correct sign for each distance according to the sign convention for mirrors.**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
##Strategy

The object is placed to the right of the mirror, so light is incident on the mirror towards the *left*.

- So, **horizontal distances** to the *left* of the mirror are positive.

- Besides, all **vertical distances** *above* the principal axis are positive.

##Solution

[[☃ image 1]]

The image, $\text I$, and the focus, $\text F$ are both to the right of the mirror.     
$\Rightarrow$ Image distance and focal length are negative.

The object height is a vertical distance above the principal axis.     
$\Rightarrow$ Object height is positive.

The correct answer is:

||
- | :-:
$\text{Image distance}$ | $(-)$
$\text{Focal length}$ | $(-)$
$\text{Object height}$ | $(+)$

assessment_items.question_content:
Uncle Gupta is using a concave shaving mirror. He positions his razor on the principal axis, and sees a magnified, virtual image behind the mirror, as shown.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Choose the correct sign for each distance according to the sign convention for mirrors.**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
The razor is placed to the left of the mirror, so light is incident on the mirror towards the right.

##Horizontal distances

- The side of the mirror $\text{\greenD{in the direction}}$ of incident light is considered $\text{\greenD{positive}}$ (right of $\text P$ in this case).
- The side of the mirror $\text{\redD{in the opposite direction}}$ of incident light is considered $\text{\redD{negative}}$ (left of $\text P$ in this case).

While light from the razor (the object) is incident to the right, the object $\text O$, and the focus $\text F$ are both to the left of the mirror. So their distances from the mirror get negative signs. 

The image $\text I$ is on the right side of the mirror, so its distance gets a positive sign.

The correct answer is:

||
- | :-:
$\text{Object distance}$ | $(-)$
$\text{Focal length}$ | $(-)$
$\text{Image distance}$ | $(+)$

options.caption: The object is labelled $\text O$, and the image is labelled $\text I$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Subira has a spoon whose concave side forms a perfect spherical concave mirror. She places a pea in front of the concave side, below its principal axis, to form a magnified, virtual image behind the mirror, as shown.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Choose the correct sign for each distance according to the sign conventions for mirrors.**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
The pea is placed to the right of the mirror, so light is incident on the mirror towards the left.

##Horizontal distances

- The side of the mirror $\text{\greenD{in the direction}}$ of incident light is considered $\text{\greenD{positive}}$ (left of $\text P$ in this case).
- The side of the mirror $\text{\redD{in the opposite direction}}$ of incident light is considered $\text{\redD{negative}}$ (right of $\text P$ in this case).

##Vertical distances

- Distances measured $\text{\greenD{above}}$ the principal axis are taken to be $\text{\greenD{positive}}$ (both object and image heights, in this case).
- Distances measured $\text{\redD{below}}$ the principal axis are taken to be $\text{\redD{negative}}$.

While light from the pea (the object) is incident to the left, the object $\text O$ is to the right of the mirror. So its distance from the mirror gets a negative sign. Its also below the principal axis, so the object height is negative.

The image $\text I$ is on the left side of the mirror, so its distance gets a positive sign.

The correct answer is:

||
- | :-:
$\text{Object distance}$ | $(-)$
$\text{Image distance}$ | $(+)$
$\text{Object height}$ | $(-)$

options.caption: The object is labelled $\text O$, and the image is labelled $\text I$.

assessment_items.question_content:
A scientist at NASA is closely inspecting the giant concave mirror at the heart of the Hubble telescope. He sees an upright, and magnified, virtual image of himself forming behind the mirror, as shown.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Choose the correct sign for each distance according to the sign conventions for mirrors.**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
The scientist is positioned to the right of the mirror, so light is incident on the mirror towards the left.

##Horizontal distances

- The side of the mirror $\text{\greenD{in the direction}}$ of incident light is considered $\text{\greenD{positive}}$ (left of $\text P$ in this case).
- The side of the mirror $\text{\redD{in the opposite direction}}$ of incident light is considered $\text{\redD{negative}}$ (right of $\text P$ in this case).

##Vertical distances

- Distances measured $\text{\greenD{above}}$ the principal axis are taken to be $\text{\greenD{positive}}$ (both object and image heights, in this case).
- Distances measured $\text{\redD{below}}$ the principal axis are taken to be $\text{\redD{negative}}$.

While light from the scientist is incident to the left, the focus $\text F$ is to the right of the mirror. So its distance from the mirror gets a negative sign. The scientist is above the principal axis, so his height gets a positive sign.

The image $\text I$ is on the left side of the mirror, so its distance gets a positive sign.

The correct answer is:

||
- | :-:
$\text{Image distance}$ | $(+)$
$\text{Focal length}$ | $(-)$
$\text{Object height}$ | $(+)$

options.caption: The object is labelled $\text O$, and the image is labelled $\text I$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The bulge side of a shiny spherical bowl is held close to a candle kept to its right. A diminished, virtual image forms behind the convex reflecting surface. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Choose the correct sign for each distance according to the sign conventions for mirrors.**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
The candle is placed to the right of the mirror, so light is incident on the mirror towards the left.

##Horizontal distances

- The side of the mirror $\text{\greenD{in the direction}}$ of incident light is considered $\text{\greenD{positive}}$ (left of $\text P$ in this case).
- The side of the mirror $\text{\redD{in the opposite direction}}$ of incident light is considered $\text{\redD{negative}}$ (right of $\text P$ in this case).

##Vertical distances

- Distances measured $\text{\greenD{above}}$ the principal axis are taken to be $\text{\greenD{positive}}$ (both object and image heights, in this case).
- Distances measured $\text{\redD{below}}$ the principal axis are taken to be $\text{\redD{negative}}$.

While light from the candle is incident to the left, the candle itself is to the right of the mirror. So its distance from the mirror gets a negative sign. 

On the other hand, the focus $\text F$ is on the left side of the mirror, so its distance gets a positive sign.

The image height is positive, since it forms above the principal axis.

The correct answer is:

||
- | :-:
$\text{Object distance}$ | $(-)$
$\text{Focal length}$ | $(+)$
$\text{Image height}$ | $(+)$

options.caption: The object is labelled $\text O$, and the image is labelled $\text I$.

assessment_items.question_content:
A car driving close to a convex parking mirror is positioned below the principal axis. A diminished, virtual image is formed behind the mirror, as shown.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Choose the correct sign for each distance according to the sign conventions for mirrors.**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
The car is positioned to the left of the mirror, so light is incident on the mirror towards the right.

##Horizontal distances

- The side of the mirror $\text{\greenD{in the direction}}$ of incident light is considered $\text{\greenD{positive}}$ (right of $\text P$ in this case).
- The side of the mirror $\text{\redD{in the opposite direction}}$ of incident light is considered $\text{\redD{negative}}$ (left of $\text P$ in this case).

##Vertical distances

- Distances measured $\text{\greenD{above}}$ the principal axis are taken to be $\text{\greenD{positive}}$.
- Distances measured $\text{\redD{below}}$ the principal axis are taken to be $\text{\redD{negative}}$ (both object and image heights, in this case).

The focus $\text F$, and the image $\text I$ are on the right side of the mirror, so their distances get positive signs.

The object height of the car is negative, since its positioned below the principal axis.

The correct answer is:

||
- | :-:
$\text{Image distance}$ | $(+)$
$\text{Focal length}$ | $(+)$
$\text{Object height}$ | $(-)$

options.caption: The object is labelled $\text O$, and the image is labelled $\text I$. [Car image](https://openclipart.org/detail/4454/beetle-car) courtesy Openclipart.

assessment_items.question_content:
A pin is placed on the principal axis in front of a convex mirror.
A diminished, virtual image forms behind the mirror, as shown. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Choose the correct sign for each distance according to the sign conventions for mirrors.**

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
The pin is placed to the left of the mirror, so light is incident on the mirror towards the right.

##Horizontal distances

- The side of the mirror $\text{\greenD{in the direction}}$ of incident light is considered $\text{\greenD{positive}}$ (right of $\text P$ in this case).
- The side of the mirror $\text{\redD{in the opposite direction}}$ of incident light is considered $\text{\redD{negative}}$ (left of $\text P$ in this case).

##Vertical distances

- Distances measured $\text{\greenD{above}}$ the principal axis are taken to be $\text{\greenD{positive}}$ (both object and image heights, in this case).
- Distances measured $\text{\redD{below}}$ the principal axis are taken to be $\text{\redD{negative}}$.

The pin is placed above the principal axis, so its height is positive according to the sign convention.

The focus $\text F$, and the image $\text I$ are to the right side of the mirror, so their distances get positive signs too.

The correct answer is:

||
- | :-:
$\text{Object height}$ | $(+)$
$\text{Focal length}$ | $(+)$
$\text{Image distance}$ | $(+)$

options.caption: The object is labelled $\text O$, and the image is labelled $\text I$.
